title: viola canina subsp. elatior
intro: Viola canina subsp. elatior, also known as the pale dog violet, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the violet family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Viola canina subsp. elatior lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and soothing respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Viola canina subsp. elatior can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
